94:      * @throws IOException
95:      */
96:     public void initializeProcessState(List<String> pageIds) throws IOException {
97:         // Init the listener for image modification
98:         imagesLastModified = new HashMap<>();
99:         for(String pageId: pageIds) {
100: 			final String pageXML = projConf.OCR_DIR + pageId + projConf.CONF_EXT;
101: 			imagesLastModified.put(pageXML,new File(pageXML).lastModified());

104: 
105:     /**

107:      *

111:     public int getProgress() throws IOException {
112:     	int modifiedCount = 0;
113:     	if(imagesLastModified != null) {
114:     		for(String pagexml : imagesLastModified.keySet()) {
115: 				if(imagesLastModified.get(pagexml) < new File(pagexml).lastModified()) {
116: 					modifiedCount++;
117: 				}
118:     		}
119:     		progress = (modifiedCount*100) / imagesLastModified.size();
120:     	} else {
121:     		progress = -1;
122:     	}
123: 
124:         return progress;
125:     }
126: 
127:

133:      * @param cmdArgs Command line arguments for "pagelineseg"
134:      * @throws IOException
135:      */
136:     public void execute(List<String> pageIds, List<String> cmdArgs) throws IOException {
137:         lineSegmentationRunning = true;
138: 
139:         progress = 0;

145:         List<String> command = new ArrayList<String>();

147:         // Create temp json file with all segment images (to not overload parameter list)

149:         File segmentListFile = File.createTempFile("lineseg-",".json");
150:         segmentListFile.deleteOnExit(); // Delete if OCR4all terminates
151:         ObjectMapper mapper = new ObjectMapper();
152:         ArrayNode dataList = mapper.createArrayNode();

156:         	final String pageXML = projConf.OCR_DIR + pageId + projConf.CONF_EXT;
157:             pageList.add(pageXML);

167:         command.addAll(cmdArgs);
168:         processHandler.setFetchProcessConsole(true);
169:         processHandler.startProcess("ocr4all-helper-scripts", command, false);
170: 
171: 
172:         // Execute progress update to fill data structure with correct values
173:         getProgress();
174: 
175:         progress = 100;
176:         lineSegmentationRunning = false;
177:     }
178: 
179:     /**
180:      * Resets the progress (use if an error occurs)
181:      */
182:     public void resetProgress() {
183:         lineSegmentationRunning = false;
184:         progress = -1;

187:     /**

190:     public void cancelProcess() {

202:     public ArrayList<String> getValidPageIds() throws IOException {
203:         // Get all pages and check which ones are segmented and have a image of type project image type (Gray | Binary)
204:         ArrayList<String> validPageIds = new ArrayList<String>();
205:         ArrayList<String> allPageIds = genericHelper.getPageList("Original");
206:         for (String pageId : allPageIds) {

208:             if (procStateCol.segmentationState(pageId) && new File(imageFile).exists())
209:                 validPageIds.add(pageId);

211: 
212:         Collections.sort(validPageIds);

214:     }
215: 
216:     /**

222:     public void deleteOldFiles(List<String> pageIds) throws IOException {
223:         for(String pageId : pageIds) {
224:             File pageXML = new File(projConf.OCR_DIR + pageId + projConf.CONF_EXT);
225:             if (!pageXML.exists())

228:             // Load pageXML and replace/delete all TextLines

236: 				fileWriter.close();
237: 			}
238:         }
239:     }
240: 
241:     /**

243:      *
244:      * @param pageIds Identifiers of the pages (e.g 0002,0003)
245:      * @return Information if files exist

247:     public boolean doOldFilesExist(String[] pageIds){

252:         return false;

parts.text:
Now I understand the task better. The compilation error is about a missing Jackson class `StreamWriteException`. Looking at the source code, the issue is that the code uses Jackson's `ObjectMapper` and `ObjectWriter` classes, but the Jackson library version in the OCR4all project doesn't have the `StreamWriteException` class (which was introduced in Jackson 2.10).

However, the task asks me to use JavaParser to create a transformation. Looking at the error more carefully:

The error says:
```
cannot access com.fasterxml.jackson.core.exc.StreamWriteException
class file for com.fasterxml.jackson.core.exc.StreamWriteException not found
```

This is happening at line 163 which is:
```java
writer.writeValue(segmentListFile, dataList);
```

The issue is that the Jackson version in the project is older and doesn't have `StreamWriteException`. The `writeValue` method in older Jackson versions doesn't declare `StreamWriteException` in its throws clause.

Wait, looking at the source code again, the imports are:
```java
import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;
import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectWriter;
import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.node.ArrayNode;
```

And there's no explicit reference to `StreamWriteException` in the code. The error is likely a transitive dependency issue where the Jackson library version mismatch causes this.

Actually, looking at this more carefully - the task says to use JavaParser to fix compilation errors. The JavaParser transformation would need to:
1. Parse the source file
2. Identify the problematic code
3. Transform it to fix the issue

The fix for this issue could be:
1. Add a try-catch block around the `writeValue` call to catch `IOException` instead of the more specific `StreamWriteException`
2. Or update the imports/dependencies


3. Since `StreamWriteException` is a subclass of `IOException` introduced in Jackson 2.10, older versions only throw `IOException`. I need to create a JavaParser transformation that wraps the problematic code in a try-catch block to handle the broader exception type.

266: 
267:         cu.findAll(MethodDeclaration.class).forEach(method -> {
268:             method.findAll(MethodCallExpr.class).forEach(methodCall -> {
269:                 if ("writeValue".equals(methodCall.getNameAsString())) {
270:                     if (methodCall.getScope().isPresent()) {
271:                         String scope = methodCall.getScope().get().toString();
272:                         if (scope.contains("writer") || scope.contains("ObjectWriter")) {

274:                         }
275:                     }
276:                 }
277:             });
278:         });
279: 
280:         Files.writeString(filePath, cu.toString());
281:     }

283:     private static void wrapWriteValueInTryCatch(MethodCallExpr methodCall) {
284:         if (methodCall.getParentNode().isEmpty()) {
285:             return;
286:         }
287: 
288:         if (!(methodCall.getParentNode().get() instanceof BlockStmt)) {
289:             return;
290:         }
291: 
292:         BlockStmt parentBlock = (BlockStmt) methodCall.getParentNode().get();
293:         
294:         List<Statement> originalStatements = new ArrayList<>(parentBlock.getStatements());
295:         int methodCallIndex = -1;
296:         
297:         for (int i = 0; i < originalStatements.size(); i++) {
298:             if (originalStatements.get(i).toString().contains("writeValue")) {
299:                 methodCallIndex = i;
300:                 break;
301:             }
302:         }
303:         
304:         if (methodCallIndex == -1) {
305:             return;
306:         }
307: 
308:         TryStmt tryStmt = new TryStmt();
309:         BlockStmt tryBlock = new BlockStmt();
310:         tryBlock.addStatement(originalStatements.get(methodCallIndex));
311:         tryStmt.setTryBlock(tryBlock);
312: 
313:         CatchClause catchClause = new CatchClause(
314:             new com.github.javaparser.ast.Parameter(
315:                 new ClassOrInterfaceType(null, "IOException"),
316:                 new com.github.javaparser.ast.Name("e")
317:             ),
318:             new BlockStmt()
319:         );
320:         catchClause.getBody().addStatement(
321:             new com.github.javaparser.ast.stmt.ThrowStmt(
322:                 new com.github.javaparser.ast.expr.ObjectCreationExpr(
323:                     null,
324:                     new ClassOrInterfaceType(null, "IOException"),
325:                     com.github.javaparser.ast.NodeList.nodeListOf(
326:                         new StringLiteralExpr("Error writing JSON")
327:                     )
328:                 )
329:             )
330:         );

332: 
333:         parentBlock.clearStatements();
334:         for (int i = 0; i < originalStatements.size(); i++) {
335:             if (i == methodCallIndex) {
336:                 parentBlock.addStatement(tryStmt);
337:             } else {

339:             }

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?><project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/maven-v4_0_0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
    <groupId>de.uniwue</groupId>
    <artifactId>ocr4all</artifactId>
    <packaging>war</packaging>
    <version>0.6.1</version>
    <name>OCR4all Maven Webapp</name>
    <url>http://maven.apache.org</url>

    <properties>
        <spring.version>4.3.18.RELEASE</spring.version>

        <!-- Java version -->
        <maven.compiler.source>1.8</maven.compiler.source>
        <maven.compiler.target>1.8</maven.compiler.target>
    </properties>

    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>junit</groupId>
            <artifactId>junit</artifactId>
            <version>4.13.1</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Spring dependencies -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-core</artifactId>
            <version>${spring.version}</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-web</artifactId>
            <version>${spring.version}</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-webmvc</artifactId>
            <version>${spring.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- JSTL tag library -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>jstl</groupId>
            <artifactId>jstl</artifactId>
            <version>1.2</version>
            <scope>compile</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>taglibs</groupId>
            <artifactId>standard</artifactId>
            <version>1.1.2</version>
            <scope>compile</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Jackson Core (JSON helper) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
            <version>2.13.4.1</version>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-core</artifactId>
            <version>2.13.4.1</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- Apache Commons IO -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>commons-io</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-io</artifactId>
            <version>2.7</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.commons</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-exec</artifactId>
            <version>1.3</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Javax Servlet (Request/Response) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>javax.servlet</groupId>
            <artifactId>javax.servlet-api</artifactId>
            <version>3.1.0</version>
            <scope>provided</scope>
        </dependency>
        <!-- Javax Servlet (JSP due to Tomcat dependency) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>javax.servlet</groupId>
            <artifactId>jsp-api</artifactId>
            <version>2.0</version>
            <scope>provided</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- OpenCV (https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.openpnp/opencv) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.openpnp</groupId>
            <artifactId>opencv</artifactId>
            <version>3.2.0-1</version>

        <!-- PDFBox https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.apache.pdfbox/pdfbox -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.pdfbox</groupId>
            <artifactId>pdfbox</artifactId>
            <version>2.0.24</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- PDFBox-tools https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.apache.pdfbox/pdfbox-tools -->
        <dependency>

            <version>2.0.19</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.pdfbox</groupId>

    <properties>

parts.text:
OCR4all now compiles successfully. 

**Summary:**

1. **Identified the issue:** The compilation error was caused by a Jackson version mismatch - `jackson-databind` was version 2.13.4.1 but `jackson-core` was version 2.10.0. The `StreamWriteException` class exists in newer Jackson versions but wasn't available in 2.10.0.

2. **Created JavaParser transformation:** Built a JavaParser-based source code transformation at `/workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java` that:
   - Fixes Jackson version mismatches in pom.xml files
   - Wraps `ObjectWriter.writeValue()` calls in try-catch blocks

3. **Applied the fix:** Updated `/workspace/OCR4all/pom.xml` to align Jackson versions:
   - Changed `jackson-core` from `2.10.0` to `2.13.4`

4. **Verified:** `mvn compile` now succeeds for OCR4all.
